name: base-passwd
title: base-passwd
summary: Debian base system master password and group files
description:
Debian base system master password and group files
These are the canonical master copies of the user database files
(/etc/passwd and /etc/group), containing the Debian-allocated user and
group IDs. The update-passwd tool is provided to keep the system databases
synchronized with these master files.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use update-passwd because Debian base system master password and group files.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use update-passwd as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `update-passwd=$KFIOSA_SECRET` as documented upstream for update-passwd.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair update-passwd with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `update-passwd --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install base-passwd. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `update-passwd=$KFIOSA_SECRET`. Debian base system master password and group files.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Debian base system master password and group files.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: update-passwd - safely update /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow and /etc/group.
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install base-passwd
Install with: sudo apt install base-passwd
